policy_statement: SoCalGas proposes increasing gas rates starting in 2009 to recover the program costs associated with its proposed Climate Action Initiative (Application A.07-08-031), subject to CPUC approval.
verbatim_text: SoCalGas proposes that 2009 be the first year that rates are increased to recover program costs proposed in this application. ... If the Commission approves this request, a residential customer's average monthly bill of 46 therms per month of gas usage would increase by 9 cents per month, from $52.22 to $52.31.
section_header: EFFECT ON GAS RATES AND BILLS
extraction_rationale: This is an explicit proposed intervention with a defined timing (first year 2009) and mechanism (rate increase) to fund program costs, contingent on regulatory approval; it is more than background context.
reasoning: This is a proposed rate increase beginning in 2009, contingent on CPUC approval, with a quantified bill impact (9 cents/month for an average residential customer). It is actionable in the sense of a financing mechanism, but it does not state a measurable climate outcome (e.g., emissions reductions, program deliverables) and the language is conditional (“If the Commission approves”). Under the strict rule for an individual climate policy, it fails the “gold standard” because there is no quantifiable climate target, even though a timeline is present.
validation_reasoning: Quantifiable target: The text quantifies only customer bill impact/cost recovery ("bill ... would increase by 9 cents per month, from $52.22 to $52.31"), but provides no measurable climate OUTCOME (no emissions reduction, participation, installations, etc.), so has_quantifiable_target='No'. Timeline: Explicit year is given ("2009 be the first year that rates are increased"), so has_timeline='Yes'. Binding mechanism: This is a proposal "subject to CPUC approval" and conditional ("If the Commission approves"), not an adopted mandate/ordinance, so has_binding_mechanism='No'. Spatial specificity: No explicit jurisdictional scope like citywide/service territory is stated in the provided excerpt; it only references SoCalGas generally, so has_spatial_specificity='No'. Weak language: Conditional phrasing indicates non-commitment ("proposes", "If the Commission approves"), so weak_language_detected='Yes'. Strong language: No mandatory verbs tied to hard outcome targets appear, so strong_language_detected='No'. STRICT VALID RULE fails because the gold standard is not met (has_quantifiable_target='No'), and the statement is mainly a funding/rate proposal rather than an auditable climate deliverable.
